Architecture

Academic · Interior Design · Studio 5 · 2021

SummerSomewhere — Retail Interior

An offline pop-up retail store for SummerSomewhereShop — an online fashion brand that captures the essence of travel through sustainable clothing. The design translates the brand's Mediterranean and Bohemian identity into a physical space, zoned to guide the customer from window display through to final purchase, with arches, rattan furniture, floral prints and organic clothing racks as the primary design elements.

Interior DesignRetailCommercial Pop-Up StoreBrandingBangalore
LocationBangalore
Year2021
TypeCommercial · Interior
BrandSummerSomewhereShop
StudioInterior Studio 5 · Nitte SAPD

Brand identity analysis · Moodboard — Mediterranean and Bohemian · Zoning diagrams · Customer transition strategy

Design Strategy

From Window Display to Final Purchase

The zoning of the programme within the block is designed to enhance the customer transition from looking (window display → window shopping) to buying (final billing). The already existing structural features — columns in the semi-circular region — divide the display zone from the retail zone. The corner nook becomes the trial zone, the most intimate part of the journey.

The plan is zoned into two parts: Formal for official and management purposes, and Informal for public use. The formal zone is visually sealed off from the public eyes; the informal zone has an open floor plan with visual connections throughout.

Display Racks

Designed to have different eye levels to achieve maximum visual connection. The clothing rack is enclosed with racks flowing organically alongside the user, with a tree at the last unit to enhance the shopping experience.

Display Unit

Uses arches, floral prints and sage colour as a theme. Display is made to look like an exhibition — art display glass designed to showcase clothes like an art piece.

Staircase

Designed to have an underpass with jewellery set up — acting like a small dress-up room. A large staircase running throughout becomes a visual device and circulation anchor.

Renders & Isometric

Summer, Beachy, Travel Aesthetics

The entrance has plants flanked on either side and a photobooth. The entry wall carries the brand name and logo with a clear display — a peek into the store. The visual shows the perspective of the entrance and the organic clothing rack. The vibe is mostly summer, beachy and showcases travel aesthetics.

Isometric views — clothing rack module and full store · Perspective render — entrance and organic rack · Display unit components
